title: campanula rotundifolia var. dubia
intro: Campanula rotundifolia var. dubia, also known as Harebell, is a flowering plant species native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Campanula rotundifolia var. dubia include its ability to soothe respiratory issues such as coughs and colds, reduce inflammation, and act as an antiseptic to prevent infections. The herbal preparations of Harebell include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are typically used to treat respiratory issues, skin irritations, and wounds.
